req.title: Wire transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Wire Transfer Two-Factor Authentication & Manual Review Tests

```python
"""
pytest test suite for wire transfer requirements:
  1. All wire transfers require two-factor authentication (2FA).
  2. Transfers above $100,000 require manual review.
  3. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum, auto
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain enums & data-classes (represent the system under test's public API)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TransferStatus(Enum):
    PENDING_2FA         = auto()
    PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW = auto()
    APPROVED            = auto()
    REJECTED            = auto()
    FAILED_2FA          = auto()


class TwoFactorResult(Enum):
    SUCCESS = auto()
    FAILURE = auto()
    EXPIRED = auto()


@dataclass
class Customer:
    customer_id: str
    name: str
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass
class WireTransferRequest:
    customer: Customer
    amount: Decimal          # USD
    destination_account: str
    currency: str = "USD"
    two_factor_token: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class WireTransferResult:
    status: TransferStatus
    transfer_id: Optional[str] = None
    requires_manual_review: bool = False
    error_message: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# System-Under-Test stub (replace with real imports in production)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD = Decimal("100_000.00")   # > this → manual review
MIN_TRANSFER_AMOUNT     = Decimal("0.01")           # smallest legal transfer


class WireTransferService:
    """
    Thin stub that represents the real service's interface.
    In production tests, import the real implementation.
    """

    def __init__(self, auth_service, review_service, audit_service):
        self._auth   = auth_service
        self._review = review_service
        self._audit  = audit_service

    def initiate_transfer(self, request: WireTransferRequest) -> WireTransferResult:
        # Guard: customer must be active
        if not request.customer.is_active:
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.REJECTED,
                error_message="Customer account is inactive.",
            )

        # Guard: minimum transfer amount
        if request.amount < MIN_TRANSFER_AMOUNT:
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.REJECTED,
                error_message=f"Amount below minimum ({MIN_TRANSFER_AMOUNT}).",
            )

        # Step 1 – always require 2FA
        two_fa_result = self._auth.verify_two_factor(
            customer_id=request.customer.customer_id,
            token=request.two_factor_token,
        )

        if two_fa_result != TwoFactorResult.SUCCESS:
            self._audit.record_failed_2fa(request.customer.customer_id)
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.FAILED_2FA,
                error_message="Two-factor authentication failed.",
            )

        # Step 2 – manual review gate
        requires_review = request.amount > MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD

        if requires_review:
            review_id = self._review.submit_for_review(request)
            self._audit.record_review_submitted(review_id, request)
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW,
                transfer_id=review_id,
                requires_manual_review=True,
            )

        # Step 3 – auto-approve transfers that passed 2FA and are ≤ threshold
        transfer_id = self._auth.generate_transfer_id()
        self._audit.record_approved(transfer_id, request)
        return WireTransferResult(
            status=TransferStatus.APPROVED,
            transfer_id=transfer_id,
            requires_manual_review=False,
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pytest fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def auth_service():
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.SUCCESS
    svc.generate_transfer_id.return_value = "TXN-001"
    return svc


@pytest.fixture()
def review_service():
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.submit_for_review.return_value = "REVIEW-999"
    return svc


@pytest.fixture()
def audit_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def wire_service(auth_service, review_service, audit_service):
    return WireTransferService(auth_service, review_service, audit_service)


@pytest.fixture()
def active_customer():
    return Customer(customer_id="CUST-42", name="Alice Johnson", is_active=True)


@pytest.fixture()
def inactive_customer():
    return Customer(customer_id="CUST-99", name="Bob Smith", is_active=False)


def make_request(
    customer: Customer,
    amount: Decimal,
    token: str = "123456",
    destination: str = "DEST-BANK-001",
) -> WireTransferRequest:
    return WireTransferRequest(
        customer=customer,
        amount=amount,
        destination_account=destination,
        two_factor_token=token,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# Test Classes
# ===========================================================================


class TestTwoFactorAuthenticationRequired:
    """Every wire transfer must pass 2FA regardless of amount."""

    def test_transfer_approved_when_2fa_succeeds(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.SUCCESS
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED

    def test_transfer_rejected_when_2fa_fails(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"), token="WRONG")

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.FAILED_2FA

    def test_transfer_rejected_when_2fa_token_expired(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.EXPIRED
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"), token="OLD-TOKEN")

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.FAILED_2FA

    def test_transfer_rejected_when_no_2fa_token_provided(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        """A None / missing token must still be forwarded to auth and rejected."""
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"), token=None)

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.FAILED_2FA

    def test_2fa_always_called_before_any_other_step(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service, review_service
    ):
        """Auth must be invoked even for large amounts before review."""
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.SUCCESS
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("200_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        auth_service.verify_two_factor.assert_called_once_with(
            customer_id=active_customer.customer_id,
            token=request.two_factor_token,
        )

    def test_2fa_called_with_correct_customer_id(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("1_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        auth_service.verify_two_factor.assert_called_once_with(
            customer_id="CUST-42",
            token=request.two_factor_token,
        )

    def test_failed_2fa_is_audited(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service, audit_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"), token="BAD")

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        audit_service.record_failed_2fa.assert_called_once_with(
            active_customer.customer_id
        )

    def test_review_not_triggered_when_2fa_fails_large_amount(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service, review_service
    ):
        """Even a $1 M transfer must NOT reach manual review if 2FA fails."""
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("1_000_000.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        review_service.submit_for_review.assert_not_called()
        assert result.status == TransferStatus.FAILED_2FA

    def test_error_message_populated_on_2fa_failure(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.error_message is not None
        assert len(result.error_message) > 0


class TestManualReviewThreshold:
    """Transfers above $100,000 require manual review."""

    def test_transfer_above_threshold_requires_manual_review(
        self, wire_service, active_customer
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("100_000.01"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is True
        assert result.status == TransferStatus.PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW

    def test_transfer_below_threshold_auto_approved(
        self, wire_service, active_customer
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("99_999.99"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is False
        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED

    def test_review_service_called_for_large_transfer(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, review_service
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("150_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        review_service.submit_for_review.assert_called_once_with(request)

    def test_review_service_not_called_for_small_transfer(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, review_service
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("50_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        review_service.submit_for_review.assert_not_called()

    def test_manual_review_result_carries_review_id(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, review_service
    ):
        review_service.submit_for_review.return_value = "REVIEW-42"
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("250_000.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.transfer_id == "REVIEW-42"

    def test_audit_called_when_review_submitted(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, audit_service, review_service
    ):
        review_service.submit_for_review.return_value = "REVIEW-007"
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("200_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        audit_service.record_review_submitted.assert_called_once_with(
            "REVIEW-007", request
        )

    def test_approved_transfer_carries_transfer_id(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.generate_transfer_id.return_value = "TXN-XYZ"
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("5_000.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.transfer_id == "TXN-XYZ"

    def test_audit_called_on_auto_approval(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, audit_service, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.generate_transfer_id.return_value = "TXN-111"
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("5_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        audit_service.record_approved.assert_called_once_with("TXN-111", request)


class TestBoundaryBehaviour:
    """
    Exact boundary values for the $100,000 threshold and the $0.01 minimum.
    Uses equivalence partitioning:
      - Zone A: amount < $0.01          → REJECTED (below minimum)
      - Zone B: $0.01 ≤ amount ≤ $100k → APPROVED  (auto)
      - Zone C: amount > $100k          → PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW
    """

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Manual-review boundary  ($100,000.00)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount,expected_status,expect_review", [
        # one cent below threshold  → auto-approved
        (Decimal("99_999.99"),  TransferStatus.APPROVED,               False),
        # exact threshold           → auto-approved  (rule: ABOVE triggers review)
        (Decimal("100_000.00"), TransferStatus.APPROVED,               False),
        # one cent above threshold  → manual review
        (Decimal("100_000.01"), TransferStatus.PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW,  True),
    ])
    def test_manual_review_boundary(
        self,
        wire_service,
        active_customer,
        amount,
        expected_status,
        expect_review,
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, amount)

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == expected_status, (
            f"Amount {amount}: expected {expected_status}, got {result.status}"
        )
        assert result.requires_manual_review is expect_review, (
            f"Amount {amount}: requires_manual_review should be {expect_review}"
        )

    def test_exact_threshold_does_not_trigger_review(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, review_service
    ):
        """$100,000.00 exactly must NOT submit for manual review."""
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("100_000.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        review_service.submit_for_review.assert_not_called()

    def test_one_cent_above_threshold_triggers_review(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, review_service
    ):
        """$100,000.01 must submit for manual review."""
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("100_000.01"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        review_service.submit_for_review.assert_called_once()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Minimum transfer boundary  ($0.01)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount,expected_status", [
        (Decimal("0.00"),  TransferStatus.REJECTED),   # zero → rejected
        (Decimal("0.001"), TransferStatus.REJECTED),   # sub-cent → rejected
        (Decimal("0.01"),  TransferStatus.APPROVED),   # exact minimum → approved
        (Decimal("0.02"),  TransferStatus.APPROVED),   # one step above → approved
    ])
    def test_minimum_transfer_boundary(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, amount, expected_status
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, amount)

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == expected_status, (
            f"Amount {amount}: expected {expected_status}, got {result.status}"
        )

    def test_zero_amount_rejected_before_2fa(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        """A zero-amount transfer must be rejected without even calling 2FA."""
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("0.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        auth_service.verify_two_factor.assert_not_called()

    def test_exact_minimum_passes_2fa_check(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        """$0.01 is valid and must proceed to 2FA."""
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("0.01"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        auth_service.verify_two_factor.assert_called_once()

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Large / extreme values
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def test_very_large_transfer_still_requires_manual_review(
        self, wire_service, active_customer
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("999_999_999.99"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is True
        assert result.status == TransferStatus.PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW

    def test_maximum_decimal_precision_above_threshold(
        self, wire_service, active_customer
    ):
        """Ensure Decimal arithmetic handles sub-cent precision correctly."""
        # 100_000.001 > 100_000.00 → manual review
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("100_000.001"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is True


class TestCustomerValidation:
    """Customer account state guards."""

    def test_inactive_customer_transfer_rejected(
        self, wire_service, inactive_customer, auth_service
    ):
        request = make_request(inactive_customer, Decimal("500.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.REJECTED

    def test_inactive_customer_does_not_trigger_2fa(
        self, wire_service, inactive_customer, auth_service
    ):
        request = make_request(inactive_customer, Decimal("500.00"))

        wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        auth_service.verify_two_factor.assert_not_called()

    def test_inactive_customer_rejection_has_error_message(
        self, wire_service, inactive_customer
    ):
        request = make_request(inactive_customer, Decimal("500.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.error_message is not None


class TestTransferResultIntegrity:
    """Result objects must be internally consistent."""

    def test_approved_result_is_not_flagged_for_review(
        self, wire_service, active_customer
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("1_000.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status == TransferStatus.APPROVED
        assert result.requires_manual_review is False
        assert result.transfer_id is not None

    def test_pending_review_result_has_no_approved_status(
        self, wire_service, active_customer
    ):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500_000.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.status != TransferStatus.APPROVED

    def test_failed_2fa_result_has_no_transfer_id(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("500.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.transfer_id is None

    def test_failed_2fa_result_not_flagged_for_review(
        self, wire_service, active_customer, auth_service
    ):
        auth_service.verify_two_factor.return_value = TwoFactorResult.FAILURE
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("200_000.00"))

        result = wire_service.initiate_transfer(request)

        assert result.requires_manual_review is False


class TestCurrencyHandling:
    """Currency field is preserved on the request (extensibility guard)."""

    def test_default_currency_is_usd(self, active_customer):
        request = make_request(active_customer, Decimal("1_000.00"))

        assert request.currency == "USD"

    def test_non_usd_currency_preserved_on_request(self, active_customer):
        request = WireTransferRequest(
            customer=active_customer,
            amount=Decimal("5_000.00"),
            destination_account="DEST-EU",
            currency="EUR",
            two_factor_token="123456",
        )

        assert request.currency == "EUR"
```

---

## Design Decisions & Coverage Matrix

| Requirement | Test Class | Key Scenarios |
|---|---|---|
| 2FA always required | `TestTwoFactorAuthenticationRequired` | success, failure, expired, None token, ordering |
| Manual review > $100k | `TestManualReviewThreshold` | triggered, not triggered, review ID, audit |
| Boundary at $100,000 | `TestBoundaryBehaviour` | $99,999.99 / $100,000.00 / $100,000.01 (parametrized) |
| Boundary at $0.01 min | `TestBoundaryBehaviour` | $0.00 / $0.001 / $0.01 / $0.02 (parametrized) |
| Customer validation | `TestCustomerValidation` | inactive account rejected before 2FA |
| Result integrity | `TestTransferResultIntegrity` | no cross-contamination of status fields |

### Key testing patterns used
- **Parametrize** for boundary tables — keeps intent readable and data-driven
- **`MagicMock`** with explicit `return_value` — isolates service dependencies
- **`assert_called_once_with`** — verifies both *that* and *how* dependencies are called
- **`assert_not_called`** — prevents silent skip of mandatory steps (e.g., 2FA before review)
- **`Decimal`** throughout — avoids floating-point rounding errors at financial boundaries

# Wire Transfer Two-Factor Authentication & Manual Review Tests

```python
"""
pytest test suite for wire transfer requirements:
  1. All wire transfers require two-factor authentication (2FA).
  2. Transfers above $100,000 require manual review.
  3. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum, auto
from typing import Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain enums & data-classes (represent the system under test's public API)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TransferStatus(Enum):
    PENDING_2FA         = auto()
    PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW = auto()
    APPROVED            = auto()
    REJECTED            = auto()
    FAILED_2FA          = auto()


class TwoFactorResult(Enum):
    SUCCESS = auto()
    FAILURE = auto()
    EXPIRED = auto()


@dataclass
class Customer:
    customer_id: str
    name: str
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass
class WireTransferRequest:
    customer: Customer
    amount: Decimal          # USD
    destination_account: str
    currency: str = "USD"
    two_factor_token: Optional[str] = None


@dataclass
class WireTransferResult:
    status: TransferStatus
    transfer_id: Optional[str] = None
    requires_manual_review: bool = False
    error_message: Optional[str] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# System-Under-Test stub (replace with real imports in production)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD = Decimal("100_000.00")   # > this → manual review
MIN_TRANSFER_AMOUNT     = Decimal("0.01")           # smallest legal transfer


class WireTransferService:
    """
    Thin stub that represents the real service's interface.
    In production tests, import the real implementation.
    """

    def __init__(self, auth_service, review_service, audit_service):
        self._auth   = auth_service
        self._review = review_service
        self._audit  = audit_service

    def initiate_transfer(self, request: WireTransferRequest) -> WireTransferResult:
        # Guard: customer must be active
        if not request.customer.is_active:
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.REJECTED,
                error_message="Customer account is inactive.",
            )

        # Guard: minimum transfer amount
        if request.amount < MIN_TRANSFER_AMOUNT:
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.REJECTED,
                error_message=f"Amount below minimum ({MIN_TRANSFER_AMOUNT}).",
            )

        # Step 1 – always require 2FA
        two_fa_result = self._auth.verify_two_factor(
            customer_id=request.customer.customer_id,
            token=request.two_factor_token,
        )

        if two_fa_result != TwoFactorResult.SUCCESS:
            self._audit.record_failed_2fa(request.customer.customer_id)
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.FAILED_2FA,
                error_message="Two-factor authentication failed.",
            )

        # Step 2 – manual review gate
        requires_review = request.amount > MANUAL_REVIEW_THRESHOLD

        if requires_review:
            review_id = self._review.submit_for_review(request)
            self._audit.record_review_submitted(review_id, request)
            return WireTransferResult(
                status=TransferStatus.PENDING_MANUAL_REVIEW,
                transfer_id=review_id,
                requires_manual_review=True,
            )

        # Step 3 – auto-approve transfers that passed 2FA and are ≤ threshold
        transfer_id = self._auth.generate_transfer_id()
        self._audit.record_approved(transfer_id, request)
        retu
